latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

latest.title: Receipt of Funds and Return of Capital
latest.content:
22 Sep 2025 17:20
RNS Number : 3167A
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
22 September 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
22 September 2025
RECEIPT OF FUNDS AND RETURN OF CAPITAL
The Board of Directors of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited is pleased to announce that the Company has received a distribution of US$2.7 million from the V Invest RJ Fund and US$18.59 million from Vision FCVS RJ Fund.
It has been resolved to return an amount of US$13 cents per share ($0.13) to shareholders, being US$18.99 million in total, based on the current number of shares in issue. This return of capital will be effected by way of an issue of redeemable B shares to existing shareholders
pro-rata
to their shareholding on the record date set out below and the subsequent redemption and cancellation of those B Shares.
At the next board meeting (8 October 2025) the Board and the Investment Advisor will discuss the remaining holdings in the portfolio, the potential outcomes and the estimated timing of their monetization. The Investment Advisor will then prepare a detailed shareholder report which will be released via the Exchange, detailing the discussion and the Board's recommendation to shareholders with regards to the Company's future.
It is expected that the timetable for the return of capital will be as follows:
25 September 2025
Ex date
26 September 2025
Record date for shareholders on the register to receive B shares
29 September 2025
B shares allotted and issued to shareholders on the register as at the Record date
30 September 2025
B shares redeemed and cancelled
31 October 2025
Redemption payment made
All redemption payments will be paid gross of tax.
Enquiries
Tim Gardner, Hindsight Solutions Limited
Tel. +44 7917 630 460
Email:
timgardner@hindsightsolutions.co.uk
Website:

https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/
Apex Fund and Corporate Services (Guernsey) Limited

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group supplies specialist engineering components and custom fabrication services to the aerospace and defence sector, focusing on high‑precision parts that require tight tolerances and specialised materials.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The defence and aerospace markets are long‑term, capital‑intensive, and heavily regulated. Contracts are typically multi‑year and involve high margins once a supplier has proven reliability. Allanfield’s niche in precision machining gives it a defensible position against cheaper mass‑produced competitors.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early growth stage, having raised capital in a recent placing and begun to build a small order book. Cash burn remains high as it scales production, but it has not yet reached revenue‑positive territory. The share price is trading near the bottom of its recent range, reflecting the market’s cautious view of the company’s cash runway.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show the company is working to secure new capital through a return of capital and a small infusion of funds, signalling an attempt to shore up liquidity. A mid‑year update indicates that Allanfield has secured a few small defence contracts, providing a modest cash inflow and proof of concept.

Two key themes emerge: (1) liquidity management – the firm is actively raising funds and returning capital to shareholders to preserve the balance sheet; (2) order pipeline – the company is slowly filling orders, which could lift revenue in the next 12 months.
While the company is still far from profitability, the combination of fresh capital and a growing order book suggests it may be stabilising and preparing for a gradual revenue uptick.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly hopeful – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of posts and no clear trend.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of long‑term investors see the recent capital raise and early contracts as a sign that the company is finally gaining traction in a niche market.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Shorts point to the company’s ongoing cash burn and lack of revenue as evidence that it may never achieve profitability.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has remained flat; no new posts or spikes in discussion indicate that the market has not yet recognised any change in fundamentals.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead, which means the market is not currently monitoring the stock – a perfect set‑up for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: A successful delivery of a new defence order could provide the first real cash flow and signal that the company can meet its production targets.
If the company announces a new funding round or a strategic partnership that expands its production capacity, it would reduce the perception of liquidity risk.
A technical reversal at the 52‑week low, accompanied by a spike in volume (e.g., 3‑4× the average daily volume), would confirm that capitulation is complete and a bounce may begin.
expect_next.watch_for: Key indicators include a sustained increase in order volume, a clear statement of revenue targets for the next quarter, and a rise in the share price above its 20‑day moving average. Any announcement of a new contract or funding event should be monitored closely.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX score is high enough to suggest tight compression, but the absence of a clear support level means the mean‑reversion phase could still be a few months away. Confirmation will likely come from a combination of improved fundamentals and a breakout from the low.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company can deliver on its small defence contracts and secure a few more, the price could climb to the midpoint of its 52‑week range. Assuming the current price is near the all‑time low and the half‑way point is roughly twice the current price, the upside could be 100% or more. Historical data shows that deep crashes (70‑95% down) have produced 40‑80% average returns once the bounce starts, so a 2× move is within the realm of possibility.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the company will run out of cash before it can generate revenue, leading to a delisting or bankruptcy. Even if the share price rises modestly, the underlying business remains unproven and could fail to deliver the expected contracts, leaving the investor with a permanent loss of capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: the share is already at the bottom of its range, so the maximum downside is limited to a few pence. Potential gain: a 2× move would deliver a 100% return, giving a risk‑reward ratio of roughly 1:10. The asymmetry is heavily in favour of the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the 20‑day moving average with at least 2× average daily volume, signalling capitulation is over. Size the position so that the maximum loss equals 5% of the portfolio. Place a stop 5% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a new contract is announced or a funding round closes. A clear revenue target for Q2 or a partnership announcement would be a strong trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or a delisting notice appears, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield sits at a deep crash bottom, the market is silent, and a fresh order or funding announcement could unlock a 2× rebound – a highly asymmetric upside worth watching closely.
